question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the validate() method to accept a fraudulent stream file by supplying a collection where the first signature's node has totalStake = 1 (minimum non-zero), causing getStakeRequiredForConsensus() to compute a threshold of 1 (ceiling of 1 * 1/3 = 1), so that any single VERIFIED signature with stake >= 1 passes consensus, allowing a single compromised node to unilaterally accept a fraudulent file and causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ordering sensitivity of the validate() method — where totalStake is set from the first signature (line 43-47) and never updated — by reordering the signatures collection so that a node with a manipulated (low) totalStake appears first, reducing the consensus threshold and allowing a minority of colluding nodes to pass validation for a fraudulent stream file, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not verify that all signatures in the collection refer to the same stream file (only the filename from the first signature is used at line 44), so that a mixed collection of signatures from different files could cause the consensus check to pass for a fraudulent file hash that happens to accumulate enough stake from signatures intended for different files, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not validate that the totalStake reported by the first node is consistent with the sum of individual node stakes in the collection, so that a node can report an arbitrarily low totalStake while other nodes report high individual stakes, causing the threshold to be set too low and a fraudulent file to pass consensus, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a NullPointerException in the validate() method at line 45 (signature.getNode().getTotalStake()) by supplying a StreamFileSignature with a null node reference, which if uncaught propagates up the call stack and bypasses the SignatureVerificationException handler in the caller, potentially causing the stream file to be processed without proper consensus validation and leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method uses HashMultimap.create() (line 37) which is backed by HashMap and HashSet, and by crafting StreamFileSignature objects whose getFileHashAsHex() returns strings with hash collisions in the HashMap, cause O(n^2) performance degradation that makes the consensus validation so slow that legitimate stream files time out and are not ingested, causing a denial-of-service that prevents transaction recording and indirectly causes loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method sets CONSENSUS_REACHED on all signatures in the winning hash group (line 72) regardless of whether those signatures were from nodes that actually agreed on the file, so that signatures from nodes that signed different files but happen to share the same fileHash all get marked CONSENSUS_REACHED, causing downstream processing to trust signatures from nodes that did not actually validate the specific file, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not check whether the number of distinct nodes in the winning hash group meets a minimum count (only stake is checked), so that a single node with stake >= stakeRequiredForConsensus can unilaterally pass consensus, and if that node is compromised, a fraudulent stream file is accepted, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not verify that the signatures collection contains signatures from a minimum number of distinct nodes before computing consensus, so that a collection with only one signature from one node (with sufficient stake) passes validation, allowing a single compromised node to dictate the accepted stream file and causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not cross-check the totalStake value from the first node against a trusted external source (such as the NodeStakeRepository), so that a node can report a falsified totalStake that lowers the consensus threshold, and if the mirror node's address book is stale or compromised, a fraudulent stream file passes consensus and causes direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method processes signatures in the order returned by the Collection iterator, and since the collection type is not specified (just Collection<StreamFileSignature>), a caller could pass a LinkedList or other ordered collection where the attacker controls the first element, ensuring a low-totalStake node is processed first and the consensus threshold is minimized, allowing a fraudulent file to pass and causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not validate the SignatureStatus transition (a signature could already be in CONSENSUS_REACHED status before validate() is called), so that pre-marked CONSENSUS_REACHED signatures are not counted in signatureHashMap (only VERIFIED are added at line 49), causing legitimate signatures to be excluded from the consensus count and potentially allowing a fraudulent minority to pass, leading to direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not validate that the fileHash in each signature is non-null and of the expected length (384 bits for SHA-384), so that signatures with truncated or malformed fileHash values are grouped together under the same hex key, artificially inflating the apparent agreement on a fraudulent hash and causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not check whether the same node appears multiple times in the signatures collection, so that a single compromised node can submit multiple StreamFileSignature objects with the same fileHash and VERIFIED status, each contributing its stake to the accumulation at line 67, effectively multiplying its stake and passing the consensus threshold with a fraudulent file hash, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that HashMultimap.create() at line 37 uses a HashSet for values, and StreamFileSignature equality is based on filename and node fields (not fileHash), so that two signatures from the same node for the same filename but with different fileHash values would be considered equal by the HashSet and only one would be stored, potentially dropping a legitimate signature and skewing the consensus result toward a fraudulent hash, causing direct loss of funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidatorImpl.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not validate that the consensusRatio from commonDownloaderProperties is within a safe range at runtime (only validated at startup via @Min/@Max), so that if the configuration is dynamically updated to a value near zero, the consensus threshold drops to near zero and a single VERIFIED signature passes validation for a fraudulent stream file, causing direct loss of funds?
